claim_verbatim: every kilogram is launched from Earth at $1400-25000/kg
check_result: Sourced; FH ~1400/kg and ISS-CRS ~25000/kg (NASA OIG IG-18-021) are correct order-of-magnitude.

claim_verbatim: Entry tier 50 t chunk ~$400M per ship, ~$370M/yr
check_result: 50 t x $1400/kg x ~5.7 = $400M implies a ~$7/kg-equiv markup band; self-labeled 'not a financial model'. Arithmetic 50000 kg x 1400 = $70M bulk, so $400M assumes premium pricing well above displacement.
recommended_edit: footnote: reconcile $400M with 50t x displacement ($70M) — gap is the premium-pricing assumption; state it

claim_verbatim: Steady state 500-1000 t chunk, $1.3-2.3B, 70% margin

check_result: FH expendable ~$150M and Vulcan-Centaur ~$110M are within public ranges.

check_result: Saturn 8.5-10 AU; light-time 71-83 min one-way. ~75-90 min correct.

claim_verbatim: 1 m ~500 kg ice boulder; 10 m ~470 t boulder
check_result: Sphere of ice (917 kg/m3): 1 m diam -> 0.52 m3 -> 480 kg ~500 kg OK; 10 m -> 524 m3 -> 480 t ~470 t OK.

claim_verbatim: B-ring radius ~117000 km, orbital velocity ~17 km/s
check_result: v_circ at 117000 km = 18.0 km/s. Pitch says ~17. Within 6%, OK. NOTE: this number contradicts the 1.5 km/s departure in C13.

claim_verbatim: Round-trip total ~13.0 km/s (impulsive table)
check_result: Sum of impulsive table = 13.0 km/s is arithmetically correct as listed, but the table uses impulsive dv for low-thrust electric legs. Campaign finding (pitch line 35, titan): continuous-thrust inbound 24.7 + outbound 29.56 vs 6.42 impulsive each way. The ~13 km/s round-trip is impulsive-frame and understates electric-leg dv by 3.8-6.3x on spiral legs.
recommended_edit: footnote/replace: state table is impulsive-equivalent; cite continuous-thrust multiplier; cross-ref framework once R-framework-matrix-parity lands

claim_verbatim: Saturn capture (low-thrust) ~1.0 km/s
check_result: titan-2 set Saturn CAPTURE at 0.8 km/s for an eccentric sub-D-ring SOI (apoapsis 3x r_titan). Pitch's 1.0 is close IF capture is into an eccentric orbit. But see C13: pitch later puts the ship in a CIRCULAR B-ring orbit, which is inconsistent with cheap capture.
recommended_edit: footnote: 1.0 km/s assumes eccentric capture orbit (titan-2 0.8); inconsistent with circular-ring-orbit framing in step 3

claim_verbatim: Saturn departure (low-thrust) ~1.5 km/s chunk-fed
check_result: INDEFENSIBLE. From a circular B-ring orbit (v_circ 18 km/s) escape alone is 7.5 km/s impulsive. Campaign anchors: 5.5 km/s (titan-2) to 7.7 km/s (titan-3 vis-viva). 1.5 is 4-5x too low and contradicts pitch's own ~17 km/s orbital velocity (C09).
recommended_edit: correct-in-place: 5.5-7.7 km/s impulsive (much higher continuous); this is the single worst number in the pitch

claim_verbatim: Inbound chunk-fed dv ~4.2 km/s; Tsiolkovsky closes at ~54% delivery @Isp 700s (66% @1000, 70% @1200)
check_result: Reproduces exactly at face value (exp(-4.2/6.865)=54.2%). But 4.2 km/s inbound depends on the indefensible 1.5 km/s departure (C12). Fix departure to 7.7 -> inbound 10.4 km/s -> 22% delivered. Add continuous-thrust accounting -> 8-28% (Isp 1000-2000). Surviving-cell delivered fraction is 17-28% (titan-2 Block-4 21.8%, Option A 17%), NOT 54%.
recommended_edit: replace: delivered fraction 17-28%; SAME ROOT ERROR as retired P-1 75% (impulsive + low Saturn-side anchor). BLOCKED for exact framework number (R-framework-matrix-parity)
